Package org.eventb.internal.core.sc.symbolTable

Class SymbolTable<E extends IInternalElement,T extends IInternalElementType<? extends E>,I extends ISymbolInfo<E,T>>

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.core.tool.state.State
org.eventb.internal.core.sc.symbolTable.SymbolTable<E,T,I>
All Implemented Interfaces:
ISymbolTable<E,T,I>, IState
Direct Known Subclasses:
ContextLabelSymbolTable, EventLabelSymbolTable, IdentifierSymbolTable, LabelSymbolTable, MachineLabelSymbolTable
public abstract class SymbolTable<E extends IInternalElement,T extends IInternalElementType<? extends E>,I extends ISymbolInfo<E,T>> extends State implements ISymbolTable<E,T,I>

  • Method Details

    • containsKey

      public boolean containsKey(String symbol)
      Description copied from interface: ISymbolTable
      Returns whether a symbol is contained in the symbol table.
      Specified by:
      containsKey in interface ISymbolTable<E extends IInternalElement,T extends IInternalElementType<? extends E>,I extends ISymbolInfo<E,T>>
      Parameters:
      symbol - the symbol to be looked up.
      Returns:
      true if the symbol is present, false otherwise

    • getSymbolInfo

      public I getSymbolInfo(String symbol)
      Description copied from interface: ISymbolTable
      Returns the symbol info for a symbol. If the symbol is not present null is returned. If this is stacked symbol table, it is searched from top to bottom.
      Specified by:
      getSymbolInfo in interface ISymbolTable<E extends IInternalElement,T extends IInternalElementType<? extends E>,I extends ISymbolInfo<E,T>>
      Parameters:
      symbol - the symbol corresponding to the symbol info
      Returns:
      the symbol info

    • putSymbolInfo

      public void putSymbolInfo(I symbolInfo) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Description copied from interface: ISymbolTable
      Inserts a symbol info into the symbol table. Multiple insertions are not allowed. The key of the corresponding symbol is symbolInfo.getSymbol(). If the symbol table is stacked, the symbol is inserted in the top level symbol table.

      This method should be used only when no exception should be raised. Otherwise, use the alternative method ISymbolTable.tryPutSymbolInfo(ISymbolInfo).

      Specified by:
      putSymbolInfo in interface ISymbolTable<E extends IInternalElement,T extends IInternalElementType<? extends E>,I extends ISymbolInfo<E,T>>
      Parameters:
      symbolInfo - the symbol info for the symbol
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if the symbol is already present in the symbol table
      See Also:
      • ISymbolInfo.getSymbol()

    • tryPutSymbolInfo

      public boolean tryPutSymbolInfo(I symbolInfo)
      Description copied from interface: ISymbolTable
      Attempts to insert a symbol info into the symbol table and tells if it succeeded. Multiple insertions are not allowed. The key of the corresponding symbol is symbolInfo.getSymbol(). If the symbol table is stacked, the symbol is inserted in the top level symbol table.

      This is similar to ISymbolTable.putSymbolInfo(ISymbolInfo) but returning a Boolean instead of throwing an exception.

      Specified by:
      tryPutSymbolInfo in interface ISymbolTable<E extends IInternalElement,T extends IInternalElementType<? extends E>,I extends ISymbolInfo<E,T>>
      Parameters:
      symbolInfo - the symbol info for the symbol
      Returns:
      true if the symbol has been added to the table, false in case of collision
      See Also:
      • ISymbolInfo.getSymbol()

    • makeImmutable

      public void makeImmutable()
      Description copied from interface: IState
      Makes the state immutable.
      Specified by:
      makeImmutable in interface IState
      Specified by:

      Overrides:
      makeImmutable in class State
